How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-07 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 CONFLUENCE flags tickers in 2+ coherent screeners within one day; edge is concentrated in validated combos, chiefly 3+ VALID screeners or SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead
SMART_FLOW × UNUSUAL_V3FULL SIZEN=168 · DSR +4.78 · promoted VALID by the May-2026 adversarial batteryThe one stock+flow pair that survived everything. Cabled in best_trades.
Any 3+ VALID screeners within ±1 dayFULL SIZEWR 81.7% · Sharpe 2.68 · p_bonf < 0.0001 (N=126, bucket-level)The bucket edge: triple agreement among validated screeners beats solos by +5.8pp WR.

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PANW leads at 934 on CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and VOLUME_LEADER, more than double AMAT (418) and more than three times UNH (279), which share the same screener set. Below those three, seven names cluster between 95 and 100, creating a hard break that makes the distribution sharply top-heavy with no middle tier. No name today matches a validated combo - matched_combo is null across the board - so the batch carries no tier-1 statistical support from the pairs battery. NEUTRAL macro at 51.74 neither confirms nor blocks the setup. PANW is the only name worth sizing, at low conviction, on score gap alone.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: VRTX (score 5, target ~$496.15 (DD20d -4.3%)).

2 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Flags unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day open-interest delta build, EMA8/21 trend alignment, and low-OI anomaly to isolate informed institutional flow.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1QQQ ↑50 Watch$707.41 -0.28%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape has only 5 resolved trades against a minimum of 20 needed for a verdict, so the 100% live win rate cannot meaningfully confirm or contradict the 68.1% backtest figure, and the WATCH status reflects thin sample size rather than any edge deterioration.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 295 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single signal - QQQ at a score of 50 on a BULLISH SWEEP, sitting in the Watch tier. Nothing cleared the 65 threshold and nothing reached 80; the score distribution is one point at the median, which is the weakest possible output from this screener. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 51.74, offering no directional reinforcement for a large-cap ETF sweep. QQQ is the only available name, but at score 50 in Watch it is a monitor rather than a trigger - hold off unless flow re-confirms on the next intraday refresh.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day Signal tier's 73.4% win rate and +3.45% average against SPY's -0.97% confirm the backtest Sharpe of 2.34, and the June cohort's 86% WR on 150 trades - the strongest monthly print in the dataset - shows the strategy accelerating into mid-2026 rather than mean-reverting toward its 68.8% rolling average.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DUK and CL tie at the day's ceiling of 77 - DUK fits the Sinclair Utilities structural +31% variance premium bias directly, while CL earns its rank through value-factor weighting as a consumer staples name carrying elevated implied vol relative to realized. NEE, KMB, and PCG cluster at 76, deepening the defensive character of the batch. Score distribution is compressed and sub-80: 40 of 45 signals land between 65 and 77 with zero clearing 80, so no name earns high-conviction sizing by construction today. NEUTRAL macro (51.74) neither pushes nor blocks a short-vol defensive trade, making this a screener-logic-only setup rather than a macro-and-screener confluence. Prefer DUK as the first entry given its direct Utilities-bias alignment and tied-top score, at moderate size only.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

SNAP (100), KMX (99), and LUV (97) lead the batch at Very strong tier, with no pattern data available to differentiate them beyond score rank. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals score 80 or above, median lands at 75, suggesting genuine compression in the MID tertile rather than a diluted field. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 51.74) neither sponsors nor penalizes a vol-structure long, leaving the screener without a directional tailwind. Take SNAP first at moderate conviction, given its maximum score of 100 and no macro headwind to fight.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is fully ceiling-pinned: median 100, 43 of 50 names score at or above 80, and all 50 clear 65, leaving near-zero differentiation across the batch. ACWI is the most meaningful name here - a put surge on the global equity ETF with stable price is the cleanest expression of the contrarian thesis, reading as broad market hedging rather than single-name event risk. CG and BBIO are the next most interesting, with CG flagging institutional put activity in a PE financial name and BBIO pointing to event-driven hedging in biotech, though both lack pattern metadata to sharpen the read. NEUTRAL macro (score 51.74) neither adds tailwind nor creates friction for the setup. First look is ACWI at moderate conviction - the signal is the most legible in the batch, but the ceiling crush and absent backtest granularity keep sizing small.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape only partially confirms the backtest: the Very strong tier delivers 75% win rate and +6.65% avg (the key number that matters for today's LAUNCH-heavy batch) while the Strong tier drags the aggregate to +1.0% against SPY's +1.6%, keeping the verdict at WATCH rather than full confirmation, though the monthly cohort recovery from February's 24.6% win rate to June's 59.9% shows the setup performs when the tape cooperates.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEWR 56% · avg +0.7% ≈ SPY +1.4% (-0.7 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 495 resolved at +20d · WR 56% · avg +0.7% · vs SPY +1.4%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 498 closed · WR TB 45% · 10% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RAMP at 95, Very strong, LAUNCH is the standout, sitting 10 points above ELV and SRTA (both 90, Very strong, LAUNCH), and all three are the only names firing the top-tier LAUNCH pattern in today's batch. The score distribution is aggressively top-heavy: 44 of 50 signals score 80 or above with the median pinned exactly at 80, meaning the screener is drawing almost exclusively from its upper register today rather than producing a diluted mix of marginal triggers. NEUTRAL macro at 51.74 neither supports nor fights this setup - pullback-to-trend-resumption trades are relatively regime-agnostic, so the tape is not doing extra work here. Preferred name is RAMP first at high conviction, SRTA as secondary given it shares the Very strong tier and LAUNCH designation with a different sector exposure.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Flags vol-of-vol climax using VVIX percentile and VIX term structure to identify sell-volatility setups at peak fear, amplified by FOMC proximity and weekend effects.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑62 Watch$743.65 -0.54%SELL VOL1.43
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape's 64% WR and +1.48% mean return edge SPY's +1.44% by essentially nothing in aggregate, but Signal-tier trades (7 trades, 85.7% WR, +2.73% avg) confirm the edge concentrates in high-score prints - today's sole Watch entry does not qualify for that bucket - and June's 33% WR at -0.58% avg contradicts the backtest VALID verdict and warns the recent tape has meaningfully softened.

🧠 Flags unusual options positioning on calm, non-extended stocks where volume exceeds 5x the 20-day mean, filtering out late-chasing signals.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 100% win rate on 18 resolved trades looks better than the 61.1% backtest, but the verdict is WATCH because resolution sits 2 trades short of the 20-trade minimum, so the tape read carries no statistical weight yet.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RSP tops today at 83 (Strong, bullish sweep), the sole name clearing 80 out of 8 signals, making it a breadth-conviction play on equal-weight versus cap-weighted momentum. MKC prints 68 (Signal, bullish sweep) and is the cleanest single-name idea, defensive staples flow in a neutral-macro tape is the kind of quiet positioning this screener is built to catch. AMZU scores 63 (Watch) but its LONG direction annotation against a BEARISH SWEEP pattern creates a conflicting read, reduce conviction there until the signal clarifies. Only 2 of 8 names score above 65 and the median sits at 59, so the batch is thin at the top and clustered deep in Watch territory. Neutral macro at 51.74 provides no directional tailwind, go RSP first at low size.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLC leads at 95 (Very Strong) with no pattern tag available, making communication services the screener's clearest signal today. XLP at 85 and XLE at 80 both sit in Strong tier, giving the batch a defensive-plus-energy tilt that sits awkwardly in a NEUTRAL macro regime scoring 51.74. The distribution is top-heavy: 6 of 11 sectors print 80 or above with the median at 80, but every pattern field is null, stripping out the confirmation layer the construction relies on for its best trades. Take XLC first given its score gap over every other name, but the missing pattern data and flat macro keep this a half-size opener.

🧠 Flags stocks down 40-plus percent from their 52-week high that print a capitulation volume spike and reversal candle, targeting mean reversion as forced sellers exhaust.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the edge directionally - 62.1% win rate and +6.2% average beat SPY by +5.6 points - but the "Strong" tier's live 31.8% win rate and -4.2% average versus the "Signal" tier's 72% win rate and +10.6% average is an inverted-label anomaly that the backtest at p_bonferroni 0.60 cannot explain away, and that divergence is the number that matters most for sizing.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HON at score 100 leads the batch in the Very strong tier with a BREAKOUT pattern, and that tier's live-tracking 89% win rate with a +17.8% average return makes it the cleanest entry on the board today. GEOS at 95 is the second-best setup, same tier and same pattern, and earns a secondary slot on the same day if fills are clean. The score distribution is top-heavy: median sits at 82, all 50 signals print above 65, and 30 of 50 clear 80, which compresses the quality gap between names but still leaves HON and GEOS clearly separated at the top. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.74, providing no regime tailwind or headwind, and a capitulation-rebound setup fits a range-bound tape better than a trending one, so the batch is contextually appropriate rather than ideal. HON first, high conviction, size per half-Kelly of 14.5%.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores stocks on 90-day exponential-regression slope times R-squared, filtered above MA100 with SPY-above-MA200 as regime gate.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

HUM is the only name worth watching, scoring 100 and clearing Very strong in a batch of 17 where the median score is 10 and just one name clears 65. PANW at 60 is the lone runner-up but lands in Watch, not a signal tier. The distribution is severely top-heavy with seven of eight names clustered below 30, which is noise-level scoring for this screener. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (51.74), which is borderline for a system that gates entries on SPY above MA200, and cuts directly against chasing the weaker names. Take HUM first and nothing else, at low conviction until the regime clears to a confirmed bullish read.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%

min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

Two signals today, which is a thin batch. NKE prints a perfect 100 (Very strong, LONG) and GIS prints 77 (Signal, LONG), with no pattern data on either name. GIS at Signal tier is the counterintuitively better setup per live tier performance (+2.6% avg, 67% wr), and that is the one name worth watching first, at low conviction only.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when 2 or more intermarket pairs breach a 2-sigma z-score threshold over 7 days, betting on mean reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

🧠 Detects stocks holding a positive earnings gap over 1 to 3 weeks, capturing institutional accumulation after an EPS surprise and analyst revision cycle.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$10.97 -1.14%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d live tape - 60.1% WR and +4.3% avg over 20 days against a flat SPY - confirms the KEEP verdict, but the June cohort's drop to 50% WR and +1.2% avg is the number that says the edge is compressing in the current tape.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 58% · avg +4.0% · beats SPY +0.0% by +4.0 pts
Last 90 days: 588 signals captured · 513 resolved at +20d · WR 58% · avg +4.0% · vs SPY +0.0%

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KEEP verdict - 74.8% win rate and +5.0% avg 20-day return beating SPY by 4.8 points - but the June 2026 cohort at 64.8% WR and +3.2% avg, coming off a weak May at 49.4% WR and -1.1% avg, shows recent cohorts running well below the aggregate, so the screen is recovering but the 74.8% aggregate number flatters the current trend.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is a complete ceiling: all 50 signals score 100, median is 100, and every name clears the 80 threshold, leaving zero quantitative separation between names. BWXT, AHCO, and CHH are among the top picks, all at score 100 and Very strong tier with pattern data null across the board, so no secondary signal exists to differentiate them. The macro regime prints NEUTRAL at 51.74, removing any macro tailwind but not actively opposing a momentum setup. With the batch uniformly top-scored, pattern-blind, and macro flat, take BWXT first at reduced size, treating the live EMA(200) reclaim as the entry confirmation rather than leaning on the score alone.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACHC, ANAB, and BV all score 95 with the PRIMED pattern, but the 90d tier breakdown is a direct warning: the Very strong cohort runs 31.6% win rate and -8.4% average return, the worst of any tier by a wide margin. The three 85-score Strong names - ABEO, ABX, ALT - sit in the tier that actually delivers, 62.5% win rate and +6.2% average in live tracking. Score distribution is top-heavy with a median of 75 and 40% of the 50 signals clearing 80, so the batch is not thin, but the weight at the top sits in the historically punishing tier. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.74 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind, and squeeze setups are idiosyncratic enough that macro provides little guidance anyway. Preference is ABEO, ABX, or ALT at moderate conviction over the headline 95-score names, where the live edge has historically inverted.

🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume across three lookback windows times price momentum across four horizons to find volume-confirmed momentum leaders in the Russell 1000.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 Detects large OTM options blocks (>$200K, 1-10 DTE) on quiet stocks, using next-day OI delta confirmation to isolate new accumulation from closing trades.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape flatly contradicts any edge: 90-day resolved trades show 37.1% WR and a mean return of -7.7% vs SPY at -1.7%, the June 2026 cohort worsens that to -10.2% avg and 33.7% WR, and the Very strong tier (avg -9.78%) actually underperforms the Watch tier (avg -5.68%), together earning a KILL verdict and rendering the old unrigorous solo Sharpe of 3.4 meaningless until a block-bootstrapped run proves otherwise.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Flags daily rejection tails at least 1.8x ATR at Market Profile value-area extremes, betting on excess reversals at structural price boundaries.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $GLRE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1GLRE ↑100●●● Very strong$16.41 -0.06%LOW2.0928.5%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape is directionally supportive but too thin to call confirmed: 5 resolved trades show 80% WR and a +4.44% avg over 20 days against a slightly negative SPY (-0.27%), tracking above the backtest's +3.18% at 10 days, and both the June cohort (100% WR, +5.33%, n=2) and May cohort (66.7% WR, +3.85%, n=3) are positive, yet the verdict stays WATCH - the key number is 5 resolved trades against a required 20.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Detects continuation entries on post-earnings gaps by requiring a weekly red-candle pullback then a close above the prior week's high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.
Top picks: $ARCT

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%

gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Detects SMART_FLOW setups that survive a volume-conviction filter, skipping entries when KVO or Force Index signals a cross or flip within three days.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

XRT is the lone signal today, scoring 64 in Watch tier on a BULLISH SWEEP pattern - a long setup that passes the KVO+FORCE filter but sits just under the 65 conviction threshold. The batch is as thin as it gets: one name, zero signals at or above 65, zero at or above 80. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.74 is neither a headwind nor a tailwind for the setup. At this level of sparsity, treat XRT as surveillance rather than a live entry, and size accordingly if forced to act.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven validity test Sharpe 0.98 N=8 CI extreme, doctrine-rejected solo Full methodology →
🧠 Catches equities with weekly RSI(14) below 30, confirmed by daily oversold crossover and candlestick reversal, targeting a contrarian mean-reversion long.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $LXU $DKL $BORR

9EOG ↑53 Watch$136.59 +1.52%18.42—NOENGULFING
10LYB ↑52 Watch$55.81 +2.22%17.8323.59NOHAMMER
11CNR ↑50 Watch$80.73 +1.98%37.9020.62YESNONE
12COP ↑50 Watch$110.53 +1.92%40.9128.23YESNONE
13DDL ↑50 Watch$2.02 -1.23%32.2625.64YESNONE
14NRP ↑50 Watch$97.76 -0.11%31.5920.08YESNONE

16ROL ↑50 Watch$43.95 +0.76%33.1624.55YESNONE
17WLK ↑50 Watch$78.17 +2.50%33.2421.33YESNONE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The backtest is doctrine-rejected with null stats, leaving the 90d live tape as the only evidence: WR 54%, avg +1.9%, +2.8 pts over SPY earns a KEEP, but the monthly cohort win rate decelerating from March's 78.7% to June's 52.9% is the key number and signals a compressing edge.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and open-interest shifts in crypto-linked ETFs to infer directional flow ahead of spot price moves.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

3FBTC ↑50 Watch$53.91 -2.92%44.68-0.2%neutral
4IBIT ↑50 Watch$35.09 -2.92%44.550.0%neutral
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

🧠 The live tape confirms edge - 64% win rate and +7.5% avg over 20 days against SPY at -0.86% across 135 resolved trades earns the KEEP verdict, but the Watch tier's 9.54% avg running more than double the Very strong tier's 3.43% is a counterintuitive inversion that the pending backtest battery will need to explain, and it matters here because today's entire batch sits in that underperforming Very strong tier.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 64% · avg +7.5% · beats SPY -0.9% by +8.4 pts
Last 90 days: 136 signals captured · 135 resolved at +20d · WR 64% · avg +7.5% · vs SPY -0.9%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 35% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight tickers hit the max score of 100 in the Very strong tier today, all flagged LONG with no pattern data available. BL and CELH stand out for cross-sector diversity at the ceiling score, but SRTY - a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF - sitting at 100 alongside pure longs is either a small-cap stress signal or a screener artifact that warrants investigation before trading. The score distribution is top-heavy with the median at 70, 41 of 50 signals above 65, and the entire top-picks list pinned at 100, meaning the screener is firing broadly but offering no internal ranking to differentiate names. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.74, providing no directional conviction. Take BL first at low-to-moderate conviction; the null pattern field across every pick removes the usual edge filter.

🧠 Detects coordinated hedge fund accumulation via quarterly 13F-HR filings, scoring NEW entries highest to surface names where multiple top funds are building positions simultaneously.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only Very Strong names in the batch, separated from the rest of the field by more than 15 points. Both are LONG with no pattern detail disclosed, so the score alone carries the conviction - reflecting coordinated new or increased allocations across multiple top funds. The distribution is top-heavy and thin at the high end: only 2 of 50 signals clear 80, 13 reach 65 or above, and the median sits at 55, meaning the bulk of the batch is unremarkable. The NEUTRAL macro regime (51.74) provides no directional tailwind, so every read here rests on positioning conviction rather than regime alignment. Take ASML first at moderate conviction - the score gap over the field is real, but the 60-to-90-day staleness baked into 13F data limits urgency.

🧠 Finds individual stocks lagging within sectors already in a late-chase or riding phase, targeting catch-up rotation once the sector bid broadens to stragglers.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 Ranks US equities (ex-Financials, ex-Utilities) by EV/EBIT ascending - score 100 marks the cheapest operating earnings relative to enterprise value today.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Zero resolved trades out of 53 live positions and an empty monthly cohort table leave nothing to confirm or contradict the backtest, which itself does not exist - the only number on the board is a top-bucket win rate of 63.6% across 22 open, unexpired positions, too immature to read as signal.

🧠 Detects Form 4 cluster buys where 3 or more insiders purchase within 30 days, scored by cluster size, dollar value, and seniority.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.
Top picks: $CTM $FCBM $FNWD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayBuysCXOClusterAvg value

2FCBM ↑60 Watch$12.65 -2.09%15✓✓$111K

4KMX ↑60 Watch$50.59 -0.90%5✓✓$254K
5LILA ↑60 Watch$7.57 +0.40%6✓✓$5519K
6LOVE ↑60 Watch$17.97 -2.50%3✓✓$163K
7WBX ↑60 Watch$5.62 +35.10%4✓✓$1245K
8COE ↑50 Watch$16.00 -0.62%4✓·$12284K

10FTH ↑50 Watch$24.49 +6.85%4✓·$297K
11GOTU ↑50 Watch$1.78 +1.14%3✓·$243K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest stats exist for comparison, but the 90-day tape - 60% win rate and +4.7% avg vs. SPY at -0.2% - earns a KEEP, with monthly cohorts running April at 44%, May rebounding to 62%, and June pulling back to 55%, confirming the edge is real but lumpy, and that 44% April print is the number that prevents full conviction.

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs requiring both positive 12-month absolute return and top relative standing vs peers, going long only when both filters pass.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Scores retail euphoria via social attention z-score, call/put spike, and parabolic price stretch, flagging names with no earnings catalyst as short candidates.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $CART $CF $CAPR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1CART ↓100●●● Very strong$47.66 -0.87%FOMO3.240.6%
2CF ↓100●●● Very strong$117.78 +2.47%FOMO1.880.6%
3CAPR ↓57 Watch$22.68 +2.07%GREED0.57-1.5%
4ASTS ↓52 Watch$77.19 +4.02%GREED0.52-4.1%
5AVAV ↓50 Watch$167.18 +2.86%GREED0.50-1.4%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With the formal backtest unvalidated at N=17, the 90-day live tape is the only performance reference, showing a 59% win rate and a +0.06% average 20-day return that beats SPY's -0.50% by 56 basis points, but the monthly cohort trend is softening materially - May's 62.5% win rate and +1.79% average fell to June's 56.7% win rate and -1.81% average, and that negative June average is the key number suggesting recent winners are not covering recent losers.

🧠 Identifies top relative-strength stocks inside leading sectors, scoring on 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.84 -0.64%Real Estate19.0%31.5%

4A ↑65● Signal$127.86 -2.50%Healthcare15.9%17.1%
5ABCB ↑56 Watch$89.70 -0.53%Financials4.9%15.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV sits below Yang-Zhang HV20, signaling the options market is underpricing realized vol expectation.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $JPM $WFC $DHR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1JPM ↑65● Signal$335.31 -1.15%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2WFC ↑65● Signal$86.39 -0.91%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3DHR ↑55 Watch$191.62 -1.32%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
4GE ↑55 Watch$361.50 -1.49%PRE_IV_RAMP9—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

JPM and WFC are the only Signal-tier names today, both scoring 65 - the screener's ceiling on this batch - as large-cap financials enter their earnings window with straddle IV lagging historical realized vol. DHR and GE sit at Watch (55) with no pattern data attached, making them secondary at best. The distribution is compressed: four signals, median 55, nothing clearing 65, zero names above 80 - a thin, low-conviction batch by any measure. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 51.74, offering no directional tailwind for vol longs but no active headwind either. JPM first on size and liquidity, though the score ceiling argues for starter sizing only.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breakdowns below Value Area Low or false breakouts above VAH, then trades the reclaim as trapped shorts or longs are forced to cover.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AMTM $ACM $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AMTM ↑65● Signal$20.71 -2.22%BEAR_TRAP20.9223.040.89
2ACM ↑60 Watch$66.56 -1.60%BEAR_TRAP66.8270.352.35
3AVGO ↑60 Watch$379.80 +2.43%BEAR_TRAP366.64382.4218.16
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no formal backtest to benchmark against (N=0 resolved), the 90-day live tape at 75% WR and avg +6.1% beating SPY by +7.1 points - reinforced by May (76.9% WR) and June (73.9% WR) cohorts both holding above 73% - is the only evidence the edge is real, and that evidence is directionally consistent but not yet statistically sealed at N=36.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects unusual put demand by flagging stocks where put IV far exceeds call IV at the 90th percentile, suggesting abnormal institutional hedging or bearish positioning.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$379.80 +2.43%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest exists to compare against, so the 9-trade live record stands alone: overall 55.6% win rate with -5.70% average return against SPY's +0.77%, monthly cohorts swinging from May's +14.6% average to June's -24.5%, and a tier split where Very strong (75% WR, +8.0% avg) and Strong (0% WR, -55.9% avg) diverge sharply, which keeps WATCH as the right call until N reaches 20.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where the IV30 minus HV20 spread spikes above 1.5 sigma on a 90-day window, identifying elevated fear premium as a candidate for volatility selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $ALNY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1ALNY ↓62 Watch$319.40 -0.68%50%32%1.56FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 44%, avg +1.6% versus SPY +1.1%) barely clears the index by 0.5 points, and the monthly cohort trend actively contradicts optimism, with April's 56% WR and +10.8% average decaying to June's 34% WR and -3.2% average - the key number being that June is running nearly 5 percentage points below the 90-day headline win rate, suggesting the screener's edge is eroding in recent tape with no completed backtest to anchor confidence.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects a 25-delta put/call skew spike above the 90-day baseline and fades it as a contrarian signal when the underlying stock price is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑75● Signal$94.23 -3.21%66%1000.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (53% WR, avg +8.5%, beats SPY by +7.3 points, verdict KEEP) is the only performance evidence available since the backtest has not cleared the 30-trade minimum, and a severe tier inversion undermines the headline - 'Very strong' names average -6% at 12.5% WR while 'Signal' and 'Watch' names drive all the returns, placing today's mid-tier batch in the screener's productive zone, though June 2026 (WR 40%, avg -2%) broke the prior two months' trend and warrants watching.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Catches failed breakouts above the value area high where trapped longs become forced sellers, generating downside momentum on the reversion.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ASML
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ASML ↓65● Signal$1757.45 +0.58%BULL_TRAP1778.181434.67
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no backtest in existence (N=0 resolved trades), the 90-day live tape is the only validity signal, and the 53% win rate beating SPY's -0.68% by 0.7 points is directionally acceptable, but the key number is tier separation - Strong names (n=2) averaged +16.3% while Watch names (n=22) averaged -3.4%, and June's cohort improving to 59% from May's 33% confirms that score tier is doing real work rather than noise.

🧠 Composite screener flagging implied volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV/HV ratio, skew, term structure, and institutional flow.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$22.06 +1.71%53%854%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.

🧠 ALTMAN screens for financial distress risk using the Altman Z-Score framework, flagging stocks where credit quality is deteriorating or recovering across leverage, liquidity, and profitability ratios.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags institutional supply and demand imbalances by comparing price spread width against volume to identify smart-money accumulation or distribution.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

There is nothing actionable here today.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
